methodsAttempted.query: site:isct.ac.jp ("生成AI" OR "生成系AI" OR ChatGPT) (学生 OR 教員 OR 授業 OR 研究) "東京科学大学"
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the official Science Tokyo policies page and the current Tokyo Science University generative AI guideline PDF, plus event and research-news pages that were not used as policy evidence.

methodsAttempted.query: site:titech.ac.jp ("生成AI" OR "生成系AI" OR ChatGPT) (学生 OR 教員 OR 授業 OR 研究) "東京工業大学"
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found Japanese and English Tokyo Tech student notices on generative AI use in learning. These official alias-era pages were treated as duplicate historical context because the current Science Tokyo guideline now covers the policy evidence.

methodsAttempted.query: site:isct.ac.jp "Tokyo Institute of Technology" "Institute of Science Tokyo" "October 1, 2024"
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the official Science Tokyo establishment notice documenting the merger with Tokyo Institute of Technology.

artifacts.summary: Official discovery was limited to university-controlled Science Tokyo and Tokyo Tech domains. Approved evidence comes from the current Science Tokyo generative AI guideline PDF and an official Science Tokyo establishment notice documenting the Tokyo Tech transition. The Science Tokyo policies page was used to verify that the guideline PDF is an official policy item. Older Tokyo Tech student notices were documented but rejected from claim evidence as duplicate historical context, and symposium or research-center news pages were rejected as event/news or research-showcase material.

artifacts.claimText: The official Science Tokyo site says Institute of Science Tokyo opened on October 1, 2024 following the merger between Tokyo Medical and Dental University and Tokyo Institute of Technology.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Institute of Science Tokyo is the post-merger institution for Tokyo Institute of Technology sources.

citation.citationTitle: Institute of Science Tokyo established on October 1, 2024
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Science Tokyo's generative AI guideline says it sets out policies and precautions to promote appropriate and safe use of generative AI at the university, and expects all faculty, staff, and students to understand generative AI and use it under appropriate judgment.
artifacts.normalizedValue: current university-wide generative AI guideline scope

citation.citationTitle: 東京科学大学 生成 AI 利用ガイドライン
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: In education, Science Tokyo's guideline says the university does not totally restrict learners' use of generative AI; the permitted degree of use, including whether it is banned, is left to course objectives, course content, and instructor teaching and assessment policies, and students should follow instructor instructions.

citation.citationTitle: 東京科学大学 生成 AI 利用ガイドライン
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Science Tokyo's guideline says users should not enter required-confidential information, personal information, or similar data into terms-of-service external generative AI services; it states that entering required-confidential or personal information into free external generative AI services is prohibited.
artifacts.normalizedValue: do not input confidential or personal information into external generative AI services

citation.citationTitle: 東京科学大学 生成 AI 利用ガイドライン
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Science Tokyo's guideline says users bear final responsibility for their generative AI use and should verify the authenticity of generated data or responses; it characterizes handing everything to generative AI and accepting its output uncritically in contexts requiring one's own thinking, consideration, or judgment as extremely inappropriate and impermissible.
artifacts.normalizedValue: user responsibility and authenticity verification for generative AI outputs

citation.citationTitle: 東京科学大学 生成 AI 利用ガイドライン
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: For courses, Science Tokyo's guideline asks instructors to teach students about generative AI limitations, consider assessment methods, and promptly reflect a course AI-use policy in the syllabus when the policy can be clearly stated.
artifacts.normalizedValue: instructor guidance and syllabus reflection for course AI-use policies

citation.citationTitle: 東京科学大学 生成 AI 利用ガイドライン
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: For research papers, Science Tokyo's guideline says journal views on generative AI use differ and individual handling is needed, including cases where the fact of use should be disclosed; it also says researchers are responsible for text and content they use in their papers.
artifacts.normalizedValue: research paper generative AI disclosure and author responsibility

artifacts.sourceTitle: Institute of Science Tokyo established on October 1, 2024
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Institute of Science Tokyo (Science Tokyo) opened its doors on October 1, 2024, following the merger between Tokyo Medical and Dental University and Tokyo Institute of Technology, and Naoto Ohtake began his duties as the inaugural president and chief executive officer (CEO).
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Institute of Science Tokyo established on October 1, 2024
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: 東京科学大学 生成 AI 利用ガイドライン
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 本ガイドラインは、東京科学大学における生成 AI の適切かつ安全な利用を促進するための方針と注意事項を示しています。これらの正しい知識を得ることにより、すべての教職員、学生が生成 AI 全般に対する理解を深め、適切な判断のもとに利用することを期待します。
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The guideline states that it sets Science Tokyo policies and precautions for appropriate and safe use of GenAI, and expects all faculty, staff, and students to use it under appropriate judgment.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: 東京科学大学 生成 AI 利用ガイドライン
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: 東京科学大学 生成 AI 利用ガイドライン
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 本学は、学習者が生成 AI を利用することを全面的には制限せず、これと共存する方針を宣言します。生成 AI の使用が許される程度(禁止の有無を含む)は、授業の到達目標や内容、授業担当教員の指導方針・成績評価方針などに委ねられます。授業担当教員の指示に従ってください。
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The guideline says learner use is not totally restricted, course-level permission depends on course and instructor policy, and students should follow instructor instructions.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: 東京科学大学 生成 AI 利用ガイドライン
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: 東京科学大学 生成 AI 利用ガイドライン
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 無償の外部生成 AI サービス（例︓無料版 ChatGPT、DALL-E、Stable Diffusion など）はセキュリティ面で十分な保証がなく、要機密情報や個人情報の入力は禁止されています。約款型外部サービスの生成 AI を利用する際には、無償版、有償版を問わず、原則として要機密情報、個人情報などは入力しないでください。
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The guideline prohibits entering confidential or personal information into free external GenAI services and says users should not enter such information into terms-of-service external GenAI services.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: 東京科学大学 生成 AI 利用ガイドライン
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: 東京科学大学 生成 AI 利用ガイドライン
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 生成 AI の利用にあたっては、利用者はその利用内容に対して最終的な責任を負うことを自覚し、必ず生成されたデータや回答等の生成物の真正性を確認してください。利用者自身の思考、考察、判断などが求められる場面において、生成 AI にすべて丸投げし鵜呑みにするような利用（レポート作成または評価、感想、所感などを生成させることなど）は、甚だ不適切であり許される行為ではありません。
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The guideline says users bear final responsibility, should verify GenAI output authenticity, and that fully delegating thinking or judgment tasks to GenAI is inappropriate and impermissible.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: 東京科学大学 生成 AI 利用ガイドライン
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: 東京科学大学 生成 AI 利用ガイドライン
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 生成 AI は誤った情報を出力することがあり、一方で個人の未発表の情報を入力しても削除できません。生成 AI の限界についてご指導ください。授業における学習者の発言や提出されたレポート、プロダクトにおいて、生成 AI がどの程度活用されたかを判断することは困難です。学習の評価方法についてご配慮ください。生成 AI の使用に対する授業方針が固まり、その明文化が可能な場合は、速やかにシラバスへの反映をお願いいたします。
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The guideline asks instructors to teach GenAI limitations, consider assessment methods, and promptly reflect clearly stated course AI-use policies in syllabi.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: 東京科学大学 生成 AI 利用ガイドライン
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: 東京科学大学 生成 AI 利用ガイドライン
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 生成 AI の研究論文作成などへの利用に関し、利用の是非や、利用の事実を明記すべきかなどの議論がなされています。学術誌によって生成 AI の利用に対する見解が異なっている点に十分留意する必要があります。生成 AI を研究論文等の作成に利用する際は、その文章、内容を自らの研究論文等に用いた場合、研究論文等における責任は自ら負う必要があることを十分認識すべきです。
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The guideline says journals differ on GenAI use and disclosure, and researchers should recognize that they are responsible for text and content used in research papers.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.title: Tokyo Institute of Technology / Science Tokyo AI Policy Evidence Draft
artifacts.summary: Staged evidence for the Tokyo Institute of Technology QS row is based on current official Science Tokyo sources. An official Science Tokyo establishment notice documents the institutional transition from Tokyo Institute of Technology to Institute of Science Tokyo. The current Science Tokyo generative AI utilization guideline supports narrow claims on guideline scope, course-level conditional GenAI use, confidential and personal information restrictions, user responsibility for outputs, instructor guidance and syllabus reflection, and research-paper disclosure and responsibility. Older official Tokyo Tech student notices were found but rejected from claim evidence as duplicate historical context because the Science Tokyo guideline is broader and more current.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Evidence is limited to official public Science Tokyo and Tokyo Tech pages available without login; no login-gated or non-official sources were used.
The entity slug remains tokyo-institute-of-technology for the QS 2026 row, while current official sources use Institute of Science Tokyo / Science Tokyo after the October 1, 2024 merger.
Claims are staged candidates only and are not marked canonical or human-reviewed.
Event, symposium, research-center news, and duplicate historical Tokyo Tech notices were not used as policy evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
